title: ADMP Exceptions and Exemptions
description: Exceptions disapply Right to Refuse and Right to Human Review (but not Right to Information). Exemptions disapply all ADM Restrictions. Source: JPDP ADMP Guideline (PCP No. 3/2025 substantive content).

exceptions.scope: Exceptions ('Kekecualian', §9.9 of issued guideline) disapply the rights that are not codified in the issued guideline (Right to Refuse, Right to Human Review). The Section 7 NOTICE obligation that ADMP is occurring remains mandatory; explanation of logic is permissive ('boleh') under §9.4 with trade-secret/IP carve-outs.

guidelineStatus.issuedReference: Garis Panduan ADMP, JPDP April 2026, Section 9.9

list.name: Contract necessity
list.rule: Where the processing is necessary for entering into, or performance of, a contract between the data subject and a data controller (§9.9.1).

list.name: Legal compliance
list.rule: Where the processing is necessary for compliance with laws (§9.9.2).

list.name: Consent
list.rule: Where the data subject has given consent (§9.9.3 — 'subjek data telah memberikan persetujuan').
list.consentDowngradeNote: PCP §2.18 proposed 'prior explicit consent' (persetujuan secara nyata). The issued guideline uses plain 'persetujuan' (consent) for this Exception. Explicit consent is required only for sensitive PD under s40(1)(a). The PCP's heightened consent standard for the general ADMP Exception was NOT adopted in the issued guideline.

sensitiveDataRule.rule: Automated decision-making involving sensitive personal data is governed by s40 of Act 709 — process only where (a) explicit consent (s40(1)(a) 'persetujuan secara nyata') OR (b) one of the s40(1)(b) conditions applies (employment law obligation / vital interests / medical purposes / legal proceedings or advice).
sensitiveDataRule.additionalSafeguards: Per issued §8.2, controllers 'boleh mempertimbangkan' (may consider) implementing technical measures (encryption, access controls) and organisational measures. The issued guideline frames safeguards as permissive, not mandatory. PCP §2.17 framed safeguards as mandatory.
sensitiveDataRule.deviationFromPCP: PCP §2.17(a) proposed 'explicit consent OR public interest'; issued guideline replaces 'public interest' with the s40(1)(b) statutory list.

exemptions.scope: Exemptions disapply ALL ADM Restrictions (including the Right to Information). Based on Section 45 of Act 709.

list.name: Personal/family/household
list.rule: Personal data processed for individual's personal, family or household affairs, including recreational purposes.

list.name: Crime prevention/detection/prosecution
list.rule: Personal data processed for: (i) prevention or detection of crime or for the purpose of investigations; (ii) apprehension or prosecution of offenders; or (iii) the assessment or collection of any tax or duty or any other imposition of a similar nature.

list.name: Statistics or research
list.rule: Personal data processed for preparing statistics or carrying out research, provided that such data is not processed for any other purpose and the resulting statistics/research are not made available in a form which identifies the data subject.

list.name: Court order or judgment
list.rule: Personal data necessary for the purpose of or in connection with any order or judgment of a court.

list.name: Regulatory functions
list.rule: Personal data processed for the purpose of discharging regulatory functions.

list.name: Journalism, literature, art
list.rule: Processed only for journalistic, literary or artistic purposes, provided that: (i) processing is undertaken with a view to publication; (ii) the data controller reasonably believes publication would be in the public interest considering freedom of expression; and (iii) the data controller reasonably believes compliance with the relevant provision is incompatible with the journalistic/literary/artistic purpose.

decisionLogic: Step 1: Does an Exemption apply (Section 45-derived)? → If YES: no ADMP Guideline obligations apply.

Step 2: Does an Exception apply (contract / legal compliance / consent)? → Note: (a) the issued guideline does not codify a Right to Refuse or Right to Human Review for those to be 'disapplied' under Exceptions — the PCP framework was replaced with s7 notice + s38 withdrawal; (b) §9.9.3 uses plain 'persetujuan' (consent), NOT 'persetujuan secara nyata' (explicit consent).
Step 3: For sensitive personal data: process only under s40 conditions (explicit consent under s40(1)(a) or s40(1)(b) grounds: employment law / vital interests / medical / legal proceedings).

Step 4: Children — the issued ADMP Guideline does NOT address children. Cross-reference dpbd/childrens-privacy.json — but note the PCP profiling/ADM prohibition for children was NOT carried into the DPbD issued guideline either. Treat as cross-jurisdictional best practice pending future Tata Amalan / Code of Practice.
